UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile on the GameFlashX platform, storing personal information, balance, and referral details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, typically linked to the authentication system's user ID.

email.description: The user's email address, obtained from the authentication system and used as their primary contact.

username.description: A unique display name for the user, typically auto-generated from their email address and read-only.

paypalEmail.description: The PayPal email address designated for withdrawal requests, which defaults to the user's login email.

availableBalance.description: The total monetary balance accumulated by the user from completed and claimed rewards, available for withdrawal.

referralCode.description: A unique code assigned to this user, used for the 'Refer & Earn' program to track their referrals.

referredByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who referred this user to the platform. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserProfile)

referralEarnings.description: The total monetary amount earned by this user specifically from their referral activities.

totalReferrals.description: The total count of other users who have successfully signed up using this user's unique referral code.

claimedOffers.description: List of unique offer IDs already claimed by the user to prevent duplicates.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user profile information was updated.

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Defines an available task that users can complete to earn rewards, such as testing apps or playing games.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Task entity.

title.description: The main title or short name of the task, clearly indicating its nature.

description.description: A brief explanation of the task's objective or requirements.

category.description: The category to which the task belongs (e.g., 'Test Apps & Earn Cash', 'Play Games & Earn Cash').

estimatedUsersMonthly.description: An approximate count of users who engage with this task on a monthly basis, for trending display.

offerLink.description: The external URL where users are redirected to perform the CPA (Cost Per Action) offer for this task.

baseRewardAmount.description: The fixed monetary reward amount a user receives upon successful completion of this task.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the task was initially created and added to the platform.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the task's details were modified.

Reward.title: Reward
Reward.description: Represents a single reward instance earned by a user, detailing its type, amount, and current status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Reward entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who received this reward. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Reward)

type.description: The category or source of the reward (e.g., 'taskCompletion', 'referralSignupBonus', 'referralLifetimeCommission').

amount.description: The monetary value of this specific reward.

status.description: The current state of the reward (e.g., 'pending' after task start, 'completed' after verification, 'claimed' after withdrawal).

description.description: A descriptive text explaining what the reward is for (e.g., 'Completed 'Play Candy Crush'', 'Referral bonus for John Doe').

associatedTaskId.description: Reference to the Task ID that this reward is associated with, if applicable (for 'taskCompletion' rewards). (Relationship: Task 1:N Reward)

originatingUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user whose action (e.g., being referred and completing a first task) triggered this reward for the recipient (for 'referralSignupBonus' rewards). (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Reward)

originatingRewardId.description: Reference to the original Reward ID that generated this commission (for 'referralLifetimeCommission' rewards). (Relationship: Reward 1:N Reward)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the reward record was created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the reward's status or details were updated.

GiftCardBrand.title: GiftCardBrand
GiftCardBrand.description: Represents a specific brand of digital gift card available on GameFlashX, used for display and SEO.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GiftCardBrand entity.

name.description: The official name of the gift card brand (e.g., 'Amazon', 'Steam', 'Netflix').

category.description: The category under which this gift card brand is listed (e.g., 'Shopping', 'Gaming', 'Entertainment').

imageUrl.description: The URL to an image asset (e.g., logo) representing this gift card brand for UI display.

seoSlug.description: A URL-friendly string used to create dedicated SEO-optimized landing pages for this brand (e.g., 'amazon-gift-card').

description.description: An AI-generated, compelling marketing description for the gift card brand, used primarily for SEO content.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the gift card brand record was created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the gift card brand's details were updated.

GiftCardValue.title: GiftCardValue
GiftCardValue.description: Represents a specific monetary denomination available for a particular gift card brand.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GiftCardValue entity.

giftCardBrandId.description: Reference to the GiftCardBrand ID that this specific value belongs to. (Relationship: GiftCardBrand 1:N GiftCardValue)

value.description: The specific monetary amount of the gift card (e.g., 10, 25, 50, 100).

currency.description: The currency code for this gift card value (e.g., 'USD', 'GBP', 'CAD').

stockCount.description: The current number of gift cards of this specific brand and value available in stock.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this gift card value record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the gift card value details or stock count were updated.

definition.description: Stores a user's profile information, balance, and referral data. The document ID (`userId`) must match the authenticated user's UID (`request.auth.uid`). Includes denormalized 'referredByUserId' for referral logic.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Publicly accessible tasks available for users to complete. Read-only for users, writable only by platform administrators.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific task.

definition.description: Records individual rewards earned by a specific user. The 'userId' in the path must match 'request.auth.uid', and 'resource.data.userId' within the document for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this reward, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: rewardId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific reward.

definition.description: Definitions of gift card brands available on the platform. Publicly accessible for users, writable only by platform administrators.

params.name: brandId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific gift card brand.

definition.description: Specific monetary denominations available for a particular gift card brand. Publicly accessible for users, writable only by platform administrators. Includes denormalized 'giftCardBrandId'.

params.name: brandId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent gift card brand.

params.name: valueId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific gift card value denomination.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC). Each document's ID (`adminId`) corresponds to a user's UID, granting administrative privileges if the document exists. Read-only for authorization checks, with highly restricted write access.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier of an administrator, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

firestore.reasoning:
The GameFlashX Firestore structure prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering strictly to the core design principles. Each user's private data, such as their `UserProfile` and `Reward`s, is segregated into user-specific paths, ensuring robust, path-based ownership. Platform-wide data like `Task`s, `GiftCardBrand`s, and `GiftCardValue`s are stored in global collections, which are publicly readable but admin-writable.

**Authorization Independence:** This design critically achieves Authorization Independence by ensuring that all authorization context is either directly part of the document being accessed or is explicitly available via the request path. For `UserProfile`s, the document ID is the user's UID, meaning `request.auth.uid` directly identifies the owner. For `Reward`s, each reward document is placed within a user's private subcollection (`/users/{userId}/rewards/{rewardId}`), and the `userId` field within the `Reward` document explicitly denormalizes this ownership. This eliminates the need for expensive and insecure `get()` calls in security rules to check parent document permissions. For instance, when a user accesses their rewards, the rule simply checks `request.auth.uid == userId` against the path and the document's own `userId` field. Similarly, when a referrer receives a bonus, a new `Reward` document is created directly under the *referrer's* `users/{referrerId}/rewards` path, ensuring their reward is independently accessible and authorized.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structure inherently supports Query-Ability and Predictable Security (QAPs) by implementing structural segregation. User-specific collections (`/users/{userId}/rewards`) ensure that `list` queries from an authenticated user will *only* return their own rewards, as the query's scope is restricted by the user's UID in the path. There's no need for security rules to filter results; the database structure itself enforces this. Global collections like `/tasks` and `/giftCardBrands` are designed for public read access, meaning any `list` query will legitimately return all available data without needing complex filtering rules. This makes rules simple, clear, and performant, typically focusing on `allow read: if true;` or `allow read: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId;`.
